Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ([[:en:1542|1542]]), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nata-shō]], [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]]. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ([[:en:1542|1542]]), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nata-shō]], [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]]. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ([[:en:1542|1542]]), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nata-shō]], [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]]. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. }}

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ([[:en:1542|1542]]), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nata-shō]], [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]]. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ([[:en:1542|1542]]), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in [[:en:Natashō, Fukui|Nata-shō]], [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]]. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] from [[:en:Emperor Ninkō|Emperor Ninkō]] onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]] active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi-Momoyama period]]. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ({{ill|1542|en|1542|WD=Q6520|lt=1542}}), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nata-shō}}, {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} from {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō}} onwards.

* Wife: Seishunin

''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} from {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō}} onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period}}. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ({{ill|1542|en|1542|WD=Q6520|lt=1542}}), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nata-shō}}, {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}}. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}

* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Kurahashi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} from {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō}} onwards.

'''Tsuchimikado Arinaga''' (土御門 有脩) was a noble and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}} active during the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} and the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi-Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}. He was the head of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the 30th head of the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家 (安倍氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿倍氏}}, which descended from the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}}.

== Biography ==
Tsuchimikado Arinaga held the Ranks of {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Hisanagi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisanagi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6867320}}. He was the head of a {{ill|dōjō family|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427847}} of the {{ill|han-ke|ja|半家|1=Half-house (Kuge)|2=ja|3=半家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/han-ke|qq=}} Rank, belonging to the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan|qq=}} lineage.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ({{ill|1542|en|1542|WD=Q6520|lt=1542|1=1542}}), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nata-shō|1=Natashō, Fukui}}, {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Q11386816|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} from {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō|1=Emperor Ninkō}} onwards.

In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 11 ({{ill|1542|en|1542|WD=Q6520|lt=1542|1=1542}}), during a period of war and instability, Arinaga left Kyoto and withdrew to his estate in {{ill|Natashō, Fukui|en|Natashō, Fukui|WD=Q6968522|lt=Nata-shō|1=Natashō, Fukui}}, {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}}. This led to his abandonment of duties as {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Onmyō no kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no kami|qq=}}. Consequently, {{ill|Kadenokōji Akitomi|ja|勘解由小路在富|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Akitomi|1=Kadenokōji Aritomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400104}}, a noble of the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} lineage residing in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, was summoned to Kyoto to take over the Onmyōryō's responsibilities.

== Lineage ==
=== Family===
* Father: {{ill|Tsuchimikado Ariharu|ja|土御門有春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Ariharu|qq=}}
* Mother: Unknown
* Wife: Seishunin
* Children:
''' {{ill|Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|ja|土御門久脩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado Hisanaga|qq=}}
''' Daughter: Married to {{ill|Kanjūji Harutoyo|ja|勧修寺晴豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji Harutoyo|1=Harutoyo Kajūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400604}}

=== Descendants===
The male-line descendants of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} through Tsuchimikado Arinaga have largely been lost. Notably, the {{ill|Kurahashi family|ja|倉橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurahashi family|1=Q11386816|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386816}} (a cadet branch of the Abe clan) and the {{ill|Kanjūji family|ja|勧修寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjūji family|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}} trace their lineage through Arinaga's children and subsequent marriages. The family line continued through daughter’s descendants and their connections to prominent aristocratic families, including the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} from {{ill|Emperor Ninkō|en|Emperor Ninkō|WD=Q312351|lt=Emperor Ninkō|1=Emperor Ninkō}} onwards.
